text.title: DRF API Testing
text.summary: APIClient usage, force_authenticate, DRF response assertions, and permission matrix parametrization.

section.title: APIClient Setup

rule.statement: Use DRF's `APIClient` for all REST endpoint tests. Create `api_client` and `authed_client` fixtures in `conftest.py`. Never instantiate `APIClient()` inside individual test functions.
rule.rationale: Centralizing `APIClient` creation in fixtures ensures consistent configuration (format, auth headers) across the test suite. Instantiating per-test hides the client setup and makes it harder to change authentication globally.

example.note: api_client and authed_client fixtures in conftest.py

section.reference: Full conftest.py with all client fixtures and factory registrations

rule.statement: Use `client.force_authenticate(user=user)` in tests — not `client.credentials(HTTP_AUTHORIZATION='Token ...')`. `force_authenticate` bypasses token validation and tests the view logic, not the token verification.
rule.rationale: Token verification is tested once in auth tests. Every other test that needs an authenticated user should bypass token parsing with `force_authenticate`. This decouples view tests from the auth backend implementation — changing from JWT to OAuth2 does not break view tests.

rule.statement: Assert status code first, then assert on `response.data` fields individually. Never assert only on `response.status_code == 200` — verify the response shape is correct.
rule.rationale: A 200 with an empty body or missing fields is a bug. Asserting on `response.data['field']` catches serializer field omissions and field name renames. Verifying `'password' not in response.data` prevents accidental credential leaks from serializer changes.

rule.statement: Use `@pytest.mark.parametrize` to test permission matrices: which roles/states produce which HTTP status codes. One parametrized test is better than N separate tests with identical structure.
rule.rationale: Permission logic is combinatorial — a ViewSet with 4 HTTP methods and 3 user roles requires 12 test cases. Parametrization makes the matrix explicit and readable. Adding a new role only requires a new tuple in the `parametrize` list, not a new test function.
